# Contributing to CLI Commands Cheatsheet

Thanks for your interest in improving this resource! This repository is the reference companion for the interactive [Command Playground](https://technoscripts.com/command-playground/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=contributing) platform.

## How to Contribute

### Submit Commands or Suggestions via Command Playground

The best way to contribute is directly through the platform — this way your submissions get synced automatically:

1. Visit [Command Playground](https://technoscripts.com/command-playground/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=contributing)
2. Pick the tool you want to contribute to
3. Use the **AI Assistant** to suggest missing commands or corrections
4. Or use the **Report** button on any question/command to flag issues

Submissions through the platform are reviewed and synced to this repository.

### Report Issues on GitHub

If you prefer GitHub, open an issue with:

- **Missing command:** Tool name + correct command syntax + brief description
- **Incorrect info:** Tool name + what's wrong + the fix
- **New tool request:** Tool name + official website + list of 10-20 key commands

### Fix Typos or Formatting

Pull requests for typos, broken links, or formatting issues are always welcome.

## Guidelines

- Keep command descriptions brief (5-15 words)
- Use the exact command syntax from the tool's official documentation
- Follow the existing file format (see any file in `tools/` for the template)
- One tool per file, named by its slug (e.g., `tools/docker.md`)

## Questions?

- Visit [Command Playground](https://technoscripts.com/command-playground/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=contributing) to explore all tools
- Reach out via [Contact Us](https://technoscripts.com/contact/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=contributing)
- Learn more [About TechnoScripts](https://technoscripts.com/about/?utm_source=github&utm_medium=repository&utm_campaign=contributing)
